2022-11-01.title: Three more grain ships set sail from Ukraine amid questions about their safety.
2022-11-01.abstract: Three more ships carrying grain departed from Ukraine’s Black Sea ports on Tuesday, a day after President Vladimir V. Putin signaled that Russia would no longer ensure the safety of the cargo vessels, a message that underscored the perils facing a watershed agreement meant to help alleviate the global food crisis.

2022-11-01.title: Here’s what’s at stake as Israelis cast their ballots.
2022-11-01.abstract: As Israelis vote on Tuesday in their fifth parliamentary election in less than four years, former Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is hoping to return to power, but polls are predicting another deadlock.

2022-11-01.title: Why Does Israel Keep Having Elections?
2022-11-01.abstract: A divided electorate and shifting alliances make for a delicate balance in the country’s voting outcomes and lawmakers’ ability to hold diverse blocs together.

2022-11-01.title: Denmark Heads for Period of Uncertainty After Snap Election
2022-11-01.abstract: With no clear coalition as of Tuesday night, the stage is set for a negotiation to form what analysts say will likely be a centrist government.

2022-11-01.title: A Bridge Offered Relief From Migrants’ Toil, Before Taking Their Lives
2022-11-01.abstract: Cash compensation for victims’ families will do little to make up for the life, and economic hope, of migrants among the 134 killed in India.

2022-11-02.title: Netanyahu Holds Slight Lead in Israeli Election, Exit Polls Show
2022-11-02.abstract: If the right-wing bloc does eke out a narrow victory, it will allow Mr. Netanyahu, Israel’s longest-serving prime minister, to return to office even as he stands trial on corruption charges.

2022-11-02.title: North Korea Launches 23 Missiles, Triggering Air-Raid Alarm in South
2022-11-02.abstract: People on an island took cover as one missile landed near South Korean territorial waters. The South responded by firing three missiles of its own.

2022-11-02.title: A Capital Draped in Darkness
2022-11-02.abstract: The streets of Ukraine’s capital, illuminated with nightlife only weeks ago, are shrouded in darkness and shadows now because of restrictions on the use of electricity. The lack of light creates moments of apprehension, but also of beauty.

2022-11-02.title: Russia rejoins the grain deal after saying it received safety assurances.
2022-11-02.abstract: Russia said on Wednesday that it was rejoining a deal that allows the shipment of grain from Ukrainian ports through the Black Sea, easing days of uncertainty over an agreement that had offered hope to countries facing severe food shortages.

2022-11-02.title: As Netanyahu Nears Victory, Israel’s Extremist Parties Celebrate
2022-11-02.abstract: Benjamin Netanyahu’s near-certain victory in this week’s election in Israel won’t be confirmed until Thursday, but his far right allies are already savoring their rise to power.

2022-11-03.title: North Korea Fires 6 More Missiles Toward Japan, Including an ICBM
2022-11-03.abstract: A day after launching nearly two dozen missiles, the North fired more. One was an intercontinental ballistic missile, but that test apparently failed, an official said.

2022-11-03.title: G7 foreign ministers meet in Germany to make plans to aid Ukraine against Russia.
2022-11-03.abstract: MÜNSTER, Germany — Antony J. Blinken, the U.S. secretary of state, and other top diplomats from the Group of 7 nations were meeting on Thursday to address global crises, with a particular focus on the allied effort to support Ukraine against the invading Russian military.

2022-11-03.title: The Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant returns to relying on generators after shelling.
2022-11-03.abstract: It is at least the fourth time the facility has lost connection to outside power since Russian forces began occupying it early in the war.

2022-11-03.title: The World Cup Is Weeks Away. Will Qatar Be Ready?
2022-11-03.abstract: As sports fans prepare to flood the tiny Gulf nation, cranes and loaders are still running hard — as is criticism of Qatar’s human rights record and exploitation of workers.

2022-11-03.title: Indian Drugs Are a Global Lifeline. For Dozens of Children, They Were Deadly.
2022-11-03.abstract: Deaths believed to be linked to contaminated cough syrups in Gambia have brought attention to loose regulations in India and a lack of testing capacity in poor importing nations.

2022-11-04.title: Corrections: Nov. 4, 2022
2022-11-04.abstract: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, Nov. 4, 2022.

2022-11-04.title: ‘I’m Afraid for My Future’: Proposed Laws Threaten Gay Life in Russia
2022-11-04.abstract: The laws, all but assured of enactment, would intensify a crackdown on L.G.B.T.Q. freedom in Russia, which Vladimir V. Putin has cast as a corrosive Western influence.

2022-11-04.title: Kherson braces for battle as the Russian administration evacuates.
2022-11-04.abstract: KYIV, Ukraine — With Russian and Ukrainian forces apparently girding to battle for the city of Kherson, signs of Kremlin rule are disappearing from the city’s streets while the remaining residents, unsure what to believe and afraid of what comes next, are stocking up on food and fuel to survive combat.

2022-11-04.title: What a Job Posting That Went Viral Says About New Zealand
2022-11-04.abstract: The country is popularly viewed as a place where you can get away from it all, but that doesn’t mean it is without its own entrenched problems.

2022-11-04.title: For Ghana’s Only Openly Transgender Musician, ‘Every Day Is Dangerous’
2022-11-04.abstract: Maxine Angel Opoku has found a new audience for her music with songs opposing a proposed law that would make it illegal to identify as gay, transgender or queer.

2022-11-05.title: The U.S. and Europe seek to assure Ukraine of support.
2022-11-05.abstract: With soaring inflation and energy prices in the United States and Europe stoking concerns in Ukraine that support for the war could wane, the United States and its allies sought this past week to offer Ukraine strong assurances that their military and financial commitment to the war effort would not waver.

2022-11-05.title: What Can U.S. Democracy Learn From Brazil?
2022-11-05.abstract: Both nations had presidents who attacked their elections. But their responses — and the aftermaths — were much different.

2022-11-05.title: A Vibrant Refuge in Seoul Goes Quiet After Deadly Crowd Crush
2022-11-05.abstract: Itaewon was popular for its diversity and nightlife. A week after a disaster killed more than 150 young people there, the neighborhood is a monument of grief.

2022-11-05.title: These Men Fled War in Syria to Ukraine. Now They’ve Fled Again.
2022-11-05.abstract: Two men displaced from Syria as civil war gripped their country spent a decade living in limbo in Ukraine. But when war came to them again, they were forced to leave, eventually making their way to London.

2022-11-05.title: Hosting Climate Summit Is Both Opportunity and Risk for Egypt
2022-11-05.abstract: The COP27 meeting next week allows a debt-wracked African country to champion climate needs of poorer nations. But it also puts Egypt under scrutiny.

2022-11-06.title: Southeast Asia is a case study in Russia’s declining prospects as an arms exporter.
2022-11-06.abstract: Analysts say Russia’s performance in Ukraine, coupled with Western sanctions, is likely to hurt its ability to sell weapons abroad. Southeast Asia is a case in point.

2022-11-06.title: Could India Help Broker Peace in Ukraine?
2022-11-06.abstract: India has good relations with both Russia and the West, and seeks a more muscular role in geopolitics. The biggest obstacle is that Ukrainians and Russians don’t want to talk.

2022-11-06.title: Somalis Are Going Hungry. Their Government Isn’t Calling It a Famine.
2022-11-06.abstract: Humanitarian groups say Somalia’s leaders are resisting a formal declaration of famine that could unlock aid and save lives.

2022-11-06.title: Taiwan’s Bomb Shelters: ‘A Space for Life. And a Space for Death.’
2022-11-06.abstract: Preparing for war over hundreds of years has left a mark on the island, with its hundreds of bomb shelters. Some are being turned into cultural oases.

2022-11-06.title: Ela Bhatt, Advocate for Women Workers in India, Dies at 89
2022-11-06.abstract: The cooperative she founded has helped millions of women who had never before had a safety net. “They were,” she said, “the poorest among the poor.”

2022-11-07.title: Even as Challenges Mount, Europeans Stick by Ukraine
2022-11-07.abstract: Inflation and anxiety over nuclear weapons may be eating into some popular support for the war, but key governments remain insulated from the pressures for now.

2022-11-07.title: A Messy Marital Split Tarnishes a Roman Soccer Legend
2022-11-07.abstract: Francesco Totti and Ilary Blasi once shared a storybook love. Now she’s hiding his Rolexes, and he’s holding hostage her Jimmy Choos.

2022-11-07.title: Egypt’s most prominent political prisoner escalates a hunger strike as the summit begins.
2022-11-07.abstract: Alaa Abd El Fattah, an activist and software developer, has been imprisoned for most of the last nine years over his condemnations of Egypt’s authoritarian government.

2022-11-07.title: Why Control of Kherson City Is Important to Russia and Ukraine
2022-11-07.abstract: The forced withdrawal of troops is a significant setback to Moscow’s campaign to control southern Ukraine.

2022-11-07.title: Italy Blocks Male Migrants From Leaving Rescue Ships
2022-11-07.abstract: The new right-wing government appears to be taking a harder line with people who are picked up in the Mediterranean as they try to cross from Libya.

2022-11-08.title: As Ukraine Bears Down, Life Worsens in Occupied Areas
2022-11-08.abstract: Those fleeing speak of the growing dangers of bombardment and of Russian forces commandeering homes and sheltering among civilians.

2022-11-08.title: Rotted Vegetables and Rancid Milk No More, as a Bridge Replaces Ferries
2022-11-08.abstract: Truck drivers used to wait for days before crossing the Gambia River by ferry, but a towering bridge has cut the transit time down to minutes, and brought Senegal and Gambia closer.

2022-11-08.title: Kenya Discloses Part of Secret Railway Contract With China
2022-11-08.abstract: The publication of details of a 2014 loan agreement for a cross-country train fulfilled a campaign vow by President William Ruto and revealed Beijing’s tough terms.

2022-11-08.title: Ocean-Eaten Islands, Fire-Scarred Forests: Our Changing World in Pictures
2022-11-08.abstract: No single image tells the story of a climate in crisis, but our photographers have come back from their global travels with an encyclopedia’s worth of pictures that hint at the scope and devastation.

2022-11-08.title: The World’s Democracies Ask: Why Can’t America Fix Itself?
2022-11-08.abstract: Conversations across continents reveal alarm over the United States’ direction, as it slides away from ideals it once pressed other nations to adopt.

2022-11-09.title: Phantom Retreats and Stolen Bones: The War of Deceit in Ukraine
2022-11-09.abstract: Both Russia and Ukraine are using confusion as well as artillery on the battlefield. The latest question: Is Russia really retreating from the strategic city of Kherson, as its defense minister says, or is it laying a trap?

2022-11-09.title: A Girl Loses Her Mother in the Jungle, and a Migrant Dream Dies
2022-11-09.abstract: The pandemic, climate change and growing conflict are forcing a seismic shift in global migration. In few places is that more clear than a perilous crossing called the Darién Gap.

2022-11-09.title: Sea Turtle Sanctuary Has Survived 40 Years. Climate Change May Kill It.
2022-11-09.abstract: Against long odds and initially strong opposition, a pristine marine preserve in the Philippines has thrived for decades under the care of local fishermen. Warming waters threaten the achievement.

2022-11-09.title: Migrant Ship, Turned Away by Italy, Heads Toward France
2022-11-09.abstract: The new right-wing government allowed passengers on three vessels to disembark, but it stood firm on a fourth, a move that France denounced as “unacceptable.”

2022-11-09.title: The Republican provocateur Lauren Boebert is in an unexpectedly tight race in Colorado.
2022-11-09.abstract: A freshman House member, Ms. Boebert, 35, was facing a stiff challenge from Adam Frisch, a Democrat.

2022-11-10.title: Amid continued military setbacks, Putin will not attend the G20 summit.
2022-11-10.abstract: The Russian delegation will be headed by Foreign Minister Sergey V. Lavrov, the RIA Novosti state news agency reported.

2022-11-10.title: Weed Is Now Legal in Thailand. How Long Will the High Times Last?
2022-11-10.abstract: Cannabis shops have multiplied since the drug was decriminalized, with caveats, in June. But some lawmakers are pushing for tighter regulation.

2022-11-10.title: A Vatican Auditor Says He Dug Up Too Much Dirt, and Was Buried
2022-11-10.abstract: Libero Milone is suing the Vatican for wrongful dismissal after he said he found cardinals siphoning off funds. The Vatican has hit him with a criminal investigation of its own.

2022-11-10.title: Egypt Starts ‘Medical Intervention’ on Prominent Political Prisoner, Family Says
2022-11-10.abstract: The hunger-striking dissident Alaa Abd El Fattah escalated his protest this week when he stopped drinking water as a U.N. climate conference opened in Egypt. His family fears that he is being force fed.

2022-11-10.title: An Outburst Threatens the French Far Right’s Effort to Go Mainstream
2022-11-10.abstract: For the first time, the National Rally is the main opposition party in Parliament. But can it use its new perch to convince voters it has changed?

2022-11-11.title: The Australian Teacher Fighting to Be the World’s Strongest Woman
2022-11-11.abstract: Elly Smith competes in a sport called strongman, where participants attempt to lift, hold, pull or carry everything from dumbbells to airplanes.

2022-11-11.title: Can There Be a Rights Reckoning for Nations That Don’t Want to Do It?
2022-11-11.abstract: In nations reckoning with atrocities, reconciliation panels and similar bodies often obscure or delay the truth. But that doesn’t mean that international justice has no effect.

2022-11-11.title: When It Comes to Bearing Bad Tidings, Putin Is Nowhere to Be Found
2022-11-11.abstract: Big decisions about Ukraine are President Vladimir V. Putin’s to make, but the Kremlin and state media are distancing him from the setback in Kherson and downplaying its seriousness.

2022-11-11.title: Zelensky says dozens of towns and villages in the Kherson region have been retaken.
2022-11-11.abstract: Russia’s announcement this week that it was retreating from parts of the Kherson region has been cast in doubt, as events have been obfuscated by the fog of war and concern rose in Kyiv that the Kremlin might be bluffing in an effort to draw Ukrainian troops into a trap.

2022-11-11.title: What I Saw in Nepal’s Forests
2022-11-11.abstract: A Times reporter spent a week in southern Nepal to understand its successful reforestation program.

2022-11-12.title: Ukraine Signals It Will Stay on the Offensive, Despite Talk of a Lull
2022-11-12.abstract: Many analysts and diplomats have suggested there could be a pause in major combat, and even peace talks, over the winter, but after pushing the Russians out of Kherson, Ukraine has no desire to stop.

2022-11-12.title: In an Era of Confrontation, Biden and Xi Seek to Set Terms
2022-11-12.abstract: Their first in-person presidential meeting, coming after both warned of deepening military, economic and diplomatic rivalry, will show how they address a range of U.S.-China tensions.

2022-11-12.title: U.K. Doubles Down on a Tactic Disproportionately Targeting Black People
2022-11-12.abstract: The United States helped inspire Britain’s tough-on-crime politics. Even as crime fell and warnings mounted, politicians never looked back.

2022-11-12.title: Justin Trudeau’s Government Sharpens Criticism of China
2022-11-12.abstract: Mr. Trudeau and his cabinet ministers are calling on democracies to increasingly turn their backs on China and other autocratic nations.

2022-11-12.title: Climate Change and Human Activity Erode Egypt’s Treasured Antiquities
2022-11-12.abstract: The effects of global warming on the country’s monuments are already striking. And the changing weather is only amplifying centuries of destructive human impact.

2022-11-13.title: While Abortion Rights Shrink in U.S., This Small Country Expanded Access
2022-11-13.abstract: The West African nation of Benin adopted one of the continent’s most liberal laws on abortion last year after hearing testimony from gynecologists about women dying from illegal abortions.

2022-11-13.title: Once Inward-Looking, Joko Widodo Casts Himself as a Global Statesman
2022-11-13.abstract: The Indonesia president, who has kept his focus close to home, is seeking a bigger role on the global stage, albeit with a domestic agenda. His next big test: the G20 summit.

2022-11-13.title: How Droughts in Mexico Could Shape the Future of the Beer Industry
2022-11-13.abstract: Brewers and other heavy water users have landed at the center of the climate fight in Mexico as the government and industry confront water shortages in the north.

2022-11-13.title: North Korea Sees New Opportunities in ‘Neo-Cold War’
2022-11-13.abstract: Kim Jong-un has launched a record number of missiles this year, hoping to leverage the tension between the United States and China, and to exploit hostilities toward Moscow.

2022-11-13.title: Israel’s Election Empowers a More Muscular Religious Zionism
2022-11-13.abstract: The extreme wing of the nationalist religious community has prevailed. At the same time, religious Zionists are increasingly part of the Israeli mainstream.

2022-11-14.title: Mehran Karimi Nasseri, Who Inspired ‘The Terminal,’ Dies in Paris Airport
2022-11-14.abstract: He slept on a bench in Charles de Gaulle Airport for 18 years, stuck in limbo as a refugee. His story inspired a Steven Spielberg film with Tom Hanks.

2022-11-14.title: Surrounded by Threats, Japan Rethinks Decades of Military Dependency
2022-11-14.abstract: As it pushes to significantly increase defense spending and develop more of its own hardware, the country may nudge the balance of power in Asia.

2022-11-14.title: Yellen Encourages Vaccinations in China as Covid Restrictions Slow Growth
2022-11-14.abstract: With China’s strict pandemic policies stalling the economy at home and globally, Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen said American mRNA vaccines are at the ready.

2022-11-14.title: Iran Attacks Opposition Bases in Iraq’s Kurdistan Region
2022-11-14.abstract: Two people were killed as security forces launched cross-border strikes at groups they accuse of fueling protests in Iran, which warned of more attacks.

2022-11-14.title: Hong Kong Demands Inquiry After Protest Song Is Played Before Rugby Match
2022-11-14.abstract: “Glory to Hong Kong,” popular among pro-democracy protesters in 2019, was broadcast instead of the Chinese national anthem.

2022-11-15.title: Accounts of Torture Emerge From Kherson, Ukraine’s ‘City of Fear’
2022-11-15.abstract: Just days after the southern city of Kherson was liberated after eight months of Russian occupation, accounts of beatings, torture and disappearances are emerging.

2022-11-15.title: Hun Sen of Cambodia Cuts G20 Trip Short After Testing Positive for Coronavirus
2022-11-15.abstract: The prime minister has met with more than a dozen world leaders in recent days, including President Biden, who tested negative on Tuesday.

2022-11-15.title: Leaders at the G20 summit urge an end to the Russia-Ukraine war.
2022-11-15.abstract: World leaders gathering in Indonesia said their nations faced challenges from conflict and economic headwinds.

2022-11-15.title: For Western Weapons, the Ukraine War Is a Beta Test
2022-11-15.abstract: Though the battle for Ukraine remains largely a grinding artillery war, new advances in technology and training there are being closely monitored for the ways they are starting to shape combat.

2022-11-15.title: What is the G20?
2022-11-15.abstract: The Group of 20 nations aims to unite world leaders around shared challenges. Here is a look at what it is and what it does.

2022-11-16.title: Poland Suggests Russian-Made Missile Killed 2 Inside Its Borders
2022-11-16.abstract: The explosion in a Polish village across the border from Ukraine occurred as Russia bombarded Ukrainian cities with about 100 missiles.

2022-11-16.title: Iran Cracks Down as Protests Show No Sign of Easing
2022-11-16.abstract: Security forces fired on a crowd at a metro station on Tuesday, setting off a panicked rush of commuters, video showed.

2022-11-16.title: Biden says the missile that killed 2 in Poland probably was not fired from Russia.
2022-11-16.abstract: It was unclear from the president’s remarks whether he meant the missile was not likely to have been fired from inside Russia, or by Russian forces in Ukraine or elsewhere.

2022-11-16.title: How an American Survived Hiding From the Russians in Kherson for 8 Months
2022-11-16.abstract: Timothy Morales, a teacher, was trapped in the southern Ukrainian city during the occupation. To evade Russian troops, he rarely ventured out, switched apartments and pretended to be Irish.

2022-11-16.title: NATO envoys gather as the explosion in Poland alarms the alliance.
2022-11-16.abstract: A day after a deadly explosion in Poland raised anxieties that Russia’s war in Ukraine could spill into the territory of a NATO member, representatives of the alliance planned to meet on Wednesday morning in Brussels to discuss the blast.

2022-11-17.title: ‘This Is Not Ukraine’s Fault’: Tensions Ease Over Missile Strike in Poland
2022-11-17.abstract: NATO and Polish officials said that it was a Ukrainian missile that crossed the border and killed two people, but that Russia bore the blame.

2022-11-17.title: Anton Filatov’s Top 10 Films for Surviving a War
2022-11-17.abstract: Mr. Filatov, who has reviewed hundreds of films for Ukrainian publications, shares a list in English of 10 films that he says has helped him cope with fighting on the front lines of the war with Russia.

2022-11-17.title: Corrections: Nov. 17, 2022
2022-11-17.abstract: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, Nov. 17, 2022.

2022-11-17.title: For Ukraine, Keeping the Lights On Is One of the Biggest Battles
2022-11-17.abstract: This week’s missile assault by Russian forces has hit at least 15 energy facilities — some for the fifth or sixth time — forcing controlled blackouts in every part of the country.

2022-11-17.title: A Dutch court is delivering a verdict in the MH17 crash, which killed 298 people.
2022-11-17.abstract: AMSTERDAM — A Dutch court is delivering its verdict in the trial of four men with ties to the Russian security services over the shooting down of a passenger jet above eastern Ukraine in 2014, during a Moscow-backed separatist uprising that foreshadowed Russia’s full-scale invasion of the country this year.

2022-11-18.title: North Korea Launches Another ICBM, One of Its Most Powerful Yet
2022-11-18.abstract: It was the latest in a series of increasingly provocative weapons tests in recent weeks, and drew condemnation from Japan and the United States.

2022-11-18.title: Corrections: Nov. 18, 2022
2022-11-18.abstract: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, Nov. 18, 2022.

2022-11-18.title: Extreme Heat Will Change Us
2022-11-18.abstract: Half the world could soon face dangerous heat. We measured the daily toll it is already taking.

2022-11-18.title: In France, Victims’ Fund Struggles to Heal Terrorism’s Traumas
2022-11-18.abstract: At the trial over the 2016 terrorist attack in Nice, bereaved family members and survivors vent frustration at a cumbersome process to obtain compensation.

2022-11-18.title: Words Were Once His Harshest Weapon. Now He Carries an AK-47.
2022-11-18.abstract: Anton Filatov, a Ukrainian film critic, was pulled into a theater he never expected to enter: the front lines of war, where he now writes of the scene in the trenches instead of what’s onscreen.

2022-11-19.title: Russia Raises Hopes for Griner’s Release, but U.S. Says It’s Just Talk
2022-11-19.abstract: The State Department says Russia refuses to negotiate seriously to free the basketball star Brittney Griner, who was sent to a penal colony with a nine-year sentence on a cannabis charge.

2022-11-19.title: Iranian Protesters Attack Khomeini’s Childhood Home as Unrest Spreads
2022-11-19.abstract: The house where the Islamic revolution’s founder grew up was set on fire during a fresh surge of anger over the government’s killing of children, including three in the city of Izeh.

2022-11-19.title: Corrections: Nov. 19, 2022
2022-11-19.abstract: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, Nov. 19, 2022.

2022-11-19.title: Kim Jong-un Takes His Daughter to Missile Test Launch
2022-11-19.abstract: Little is known about North Korea’s ruling family. Yesterday was the first public appearance of the child, who is thought to be named Ju-ae.

2022-11-19.title: Train service between Kyiv and Kherson is restored after nine months.
2022-11-19.abstract: A train, carrying 200 passengers, made the 11-hour journey for the first time since Ukraine retook the city, a senior official said.

2022-11-20.title: Malaysians Were Pitched Political Stability. Elections Created More Chaos.
2022-11-20.abstract: The country has been hit by a series of political shocks in recent years and now confronts the first hung Parliament in its history.

2022-11-20.title: As Climate Protests Get Bolder, British Police Strike Back With New Powers
2022-11-20.abstract: New rules curtailing protests have alarmed some who fear an erosion of civil rights. They come as environmental protesters have intensified their tactics to draw attention to climate change.

2022-11-20.title: In Iraq’s Mountains, Iranian Opposition Fighters Feel the Squeeze
2022-11-20.abstract: The armed groups, which have trained in the Kurdistan region for decades, face attacks from Iran and pressure from the Iraqi government to give up their weapons.

2022-11-20.title: At least 437 children have been killed in the war since February, Ukraine says.
2022-11-20.abstract: Andriy Kostin, Ukraine’s prosecutor general, said that the true numbers were likely to be far higher.

2022-11-20.title: Russia and Ukraine again trade blame for shelling at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant.
2022-11-20.abstract: The United Nations’ nuclear watchdog agency called the strikes “extremely disturbing.”

2022-11-21.title: Would You Pay $1,000 to See the World’s Biggest Lizards?
2022-11-21.abstract: Indonesia wants to make it vastly more expensive to see the country’s famous Komodo dragons. The local tourism industry is up in arms.

2022-11-21.title: Trapped Between Extremists and Extreme Weather, Somalis Brace for Famine
2022-11-21.abstract: The worst drought in 40 years is pushing Somalia to the brink. If it triggers a rare famine declaration, the militant group Al Shabab will also be to blame.

2022-11-21.title: On the River at Night, Ambushing Russians
2022-11-21.abstract: Ukrainian units plying the Dnipro River venture behind enemy lines under the cover of darkness, carrying out reconnaissance and sabotage.

2022-11-21.title: U.N. inspectors report new shelling damage at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear complex.
2022-11-21.abstract: While there were no immediate safety concerns, the head of the U.N. nuclear watchdog said the scope of the damage “demonstrates the sheer intensity of the attacks on one of the world’s largest nuclear power plants.”

2022-11-21.title: Powerful Earthquake Kills at Least 162 in Indonesia; Toll Expected to Mount
2022-11-21.abstract: The quake on the nation’s main island, Java, destroyed hundreds of buildings, and officials feared that many people were still trapped beneath the rubble.

2022-11-22.title: Ukraine Remembers a Milestone on the Long Path to Putin’s War
2022-11-22.abstract: In hindsight, protests that began nine years ago and toppled a Ukrainian president were a pivot point in efforts by Vladimir V. Putin to exert Russian control over Ukraine.

2022-11-22.title: For Ukraine, So Much Unexpected Success, and Yet So Far to Go
2022-11-22.abstract: Ukraine is on the offensive along most of the 600-mile front line, and the Russians are in a defensive crouch. But about one-fifth of Ukrainian territory is still occupied by Russia.

2022-11-22.title: Outbreaks Test China’s Efforts to Limit the Cost of ‘Zero Covid’
2022-11-22.abstract: China wants to fine-tune its stringent pandemic restrictions to reduce the disruption to daily life caused by lockdowns. But officials are still taking few chances.

2022-11-22.title: Beijing Is Tackling Its Air Pollution Problem. Why Can’t New Delhi?
2022-11-22.abstract: The return of smog season in India’s capital has again left residents asking why nothing has changed. The answer may be largely political.

2022-11-22.title: China Factory Fire Kills 38
2022-11-22.abstract: Officials say an investigation is underway into what caused the tragedy at the two-story facility operated by a wholesaler of industrial equipment, clothing and chemical products.

2022-11-23.title: Missiles for Poland Raise Questions on NATO Stance in Ukraine War
2022-11-23.abstract: NATO is determined to help Ukraine battle Russia, but wants no direct part of the war. A new promise of air defense weapons for Poland may make that more complicated.

2022-11-23.title: 2 Bomb Attacks in Jerusalem Kill 1 and Wound at Least 18
2022-11-23.abstract: The attacks prompted calls by far-right leaders for the swift formation of a new government that would be tougher on terrorism.

2022-11-23.title: Deadly Explosions Rock Bus Stops in Jerusalem
2022-11-23.abstract: The blasts occurred amid the deadliest wave of violence in Israel and the occupied West Bank since 2015.

2022-11-23.title: How Iran’s Security Forces Use Ambulances to Suppress Protests
2022-11-23.abstract: Witness accounts and video analysis reveal how the state is co-opting ambulances to infiltrate demonstrations and detain protesters.

2022-11-23.title: The Disputed Border Where Every Shepherd Is Seen as a Potential Spy
2022-11-23.abstract: Lebanese herders say that Israel crosses an ill-defined frontier to abduct and interrogate them. Israel accuses the herders of espionage on behalf of Hezbollah.

2022-11-24.title: Russian Missile Barrage Cuts Power and Water Across Ukraine
2022-11-24.abstract: Outages struck Kyiv and the surrounding region, as well as Lviv, Dnipro, Odesa and Kharkiv. Half of Moldova, whose grid is tied to Ukraine’s, also lost power.

2022-11-24.title: A U.S.-Made Missile Went Astray in Ukraine, Injuring Civilians
2022-11-24.abstract: An air-launched AGM-88B missile, built to destroy enemy radars, apparently missed its target and hit an apartment building in Kramatorsk in September.

2022-11-24.title: Pakistan Names a New Army Chief, Amid Political Drama Centered on the Military
2022-11-24.abstract: The appointment of Lt. Gen. Syed Asim Munir comes after weeks of intense speculation and backstage negotiations over who would lead the nuclear-armed military.

2022-11-24.title: Anwar, Opposition Leader for Decades, Is Now Malaysia’s Prime Minister
2022-11-24.abstract: Anwar Ibrahim, whose career has also included a stint as deputy prime minister and two jail terms, said he had the alliances to command a “convincing majority” in Parliament.

2022-11-24.title: Meet the World’s New Human Rights Crisis Manager. He Has a Lot to Do.
2022-11-24.abstract: From Iran to Ukraine to Xinjiang in China, Volker Türk will have no shortage of challenges as he steps into one of the United Nations’ most delicate roles.

2022-11-25.title: The Only U.S. Territory Without U.S. Birthright Citizenship
2022-11-25.abstract: People born in American Samoa, which has been held by the United States for more than 120 years, are not automatically citizens of the United States.

2022-11-25.title: Qataris Bristle at What They See as Double Standards Over Their World Cup
2022-11-25.abstract: Many in the country say the barrage of criticism about its human rights record and the exploitation of migrant workers is laced with discrimination and hypocrisy.

2022-11-25.title: An Israeli Schoolboy Died in the West Bank. To Find His Body, Foes Joined Forces.
2022-11-25.abstract: After Palestinian gunmen refused to hand over a dead Israeli teenager on Tuesday, officials on both sides of the conflict worked together to recover his abducted body.

2022-11-25.title: ‘Gangnam Style’ Brought K-Pop to the World, but Haunted Its Creator
2022-11-25.abstract: In 2012, the song took over the internet, and it helped pave the way for the global success of Korean pop. But Psy, the artist behind it, spent years trying and failing to replicate the phenomenon.

2022-11-25.title: He Pointed Out a Judge’s Goof. Now, He Faces Jail Time in Fiji.
2022-11-25.abstract: A lawyer who made light of a legal document’s error was convicted of contempt of court, in a sign of the island nation’s eroding civil liberties.

2022-11-26.title: U.S. and NATO Scramble to Arm Ukraine and Refill Their Own Arsenals
2022-11-26.abstract: The West thought an artillery and tank war in Europe would never happen again and shrank weapons stockpiles. It was wrong.

2022-11-26.title: They Were Surrogates. Now They Must Raise the Children.
2022-11-26.abstract: In Cambodia’s weak legal system, surrogacy exists in a gray market, endangering all involved when political conditions suddenly shift and criminal charges follow.

2022-11-26.title: Tech Turmoil Complicates Canada’s Policing of the Online World
2022-11-26.abstract: The government has four bills before Parliament to rein in tech giants at a time when the industry is retrenching.

2022-11-26.title: On the Anniversary of Ukraine’s Famine, Parallels to Russia’s Strikes
2022-11-26.abstract: The Holodomor commemoration came as President Vladimir V. Putin was accused of degrading Ukraine’s power grid to freeze the country into submission.

2022-11-26.title: Landslide on Italian Island Sweeps Away Homes and Turns Roads Into Rivers of Mud
2022-11-26.abstract: At least one person was killed and an estimated 10 others were missing on the island of Ischia, a popular tourist destination off the coast of Naples.

2022-11-27.title: Amid Israeli Blockade on Gaza, a Fishing Fleet Limps Along
2022-11-27.abstract: An Israeli blockade that restricts the movement of Gazans out of the strip and limits imports — or bans them completely — has been devastating for the enclave’s fishing industry.

2022-11-27.title: French Police Guard Water as Seasonal Drought Intensifies
2022-11-27.abstract: New reservoirs designed to supply French farms with water in increasingly arid growing seasons have attracted opposition from environmentalists.

2022-11-27.title: Beauty Over Brains: Japan’s Skin-Deep University Pageants
2022-11-27.abstract: The contests reflect a culture that often judges women by their appearance and slots them into rigid, gender-defined roles.

2022-11-27.title: He Never Denied Selling Drugs. But Britain Says He’s a Slave Master, Too.
2022-11-27.abstract: A law written to prevent human trafficking is being wielded against low-level drug dealers. The effects are long-lasting.

2022-11-27.title: After Deadly Blaze, Surge of Defiance Against China’s Covid Policies
2022-11-27.abstract: Protests became rare once the government cut off most routes to collective action. But ubiquitous Covid rules, bringing shared suffering, have created a focus for anger.

2022-11-28.title: Mexico’s President Leads Supporters in March Through Capital
2022-11-28.abstract: Tens of thousands of Mexicans walked through the streets Sunday to show their support for President Andrés Manuel López Obrador amid some pushback to his political agenda in recent weeks.

2022-11-28.title: When Visiting Michelangelo’s David, She Brings a Duster
2022-11-28.abstract: The revered statue in Italy is not going to dust itself. That’s where Eleonora Pucci comes in.

2022-11-28.title: Amid Scandals and Politics, Poland’s Youths Lose Faith in Catholic Church
2022-11-28.abstract: The Polish church is in a deep crisis, as its authority is sapped by cascading sexual abuse scandals and as more people grow wary of its perceived alliance with the country’s right-wing government.

2022-11-28.title: China Protests Break Out as Covid Cases Surge and Lockdowns Persist
2022-11-28.abstract: After a weekend of confrontations between officials and demonstrators, video from two sites in Shanghai and Beijing showed a heavy security presence. Here’s the latest on the situation.

2022-11-28.title: 3 Weeks After Hack, This Country’s Government Is Still Off-line
2022-11-28.abstract: The Pacific Island nation of Vanuatu has said little about a Nov. 6 cyberattack that has significantly inconvenienced many of its 320,000 people.

2022-11-29.title: Ukraine Warns of More Strikes on Power Plants, as Russians Dig In
2022-11-29.abstract: Russian forces are fortifying positions in southern Ukraine after retreating from Kherson, and the Kremlin denied planning to withdraw from the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant.

2022-11-29.title: Videos Show Heavy Police Presence in Response to Protests in China
2022-11-29.abstract: In Chinese cities where protests were held over the weekend, the police put on a show of force on Monday to deter gatherings. Some protesters showed up despite the tight security.

2022-11-29.title: China Launches Astronauts to Newly Completed Space Station
2022-11-29.abstract: After decades of military secrecy, Chinese officials opened their desert rocket launch center to a handful of visitors and called for international cooperation in space.

2022-11-29.title: Russian Retreat Reveals Signs of an Atrocity in a Ukrainian Village
2022-11-29.abstract: In the southern Kherson region, the pattern seen in eastern Ukraine is repeating: The withdrawal of Russian forces yields evidence of possible war crimes.

2022-11-29.title: The Woman Once Considered Africa’s Richest Faces Arrest in Graft Inquiry
2022-11-29.abstract: Isabel dos Santos, the daughter of Angola’s former president, was accused of enriching herself with state resources during her time in control of the country’s oil company.

2022-11-30.title: Protesters Clash With Police Over Covid Lockdown in China
2022-11-30.abstract: In videos verified by The New York Times, protesters in the southern city of Guangzhou confronted police officers in helmets and white protective clothing this week.

2022-11-30.title: Ukraine welcomes a U.S. push for energy aid, saying time is of the essence.
2022-11-30.abstract: BUCHAREST, Romania — Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken met on Wednesday with his Ukrainian counterpart on the sidelines of a meeting of NATO foreign ministers as the United States rushed to raise funds to help Ukraine repair energy infrastructure that has been battered by Russian attacks.

2022-11-30.title: Jiang Zemin, Leader Who Guided China Into Global Market, Dies at 96
2022-11-30.abstract: Mr. Jiang, a wily and garrulous politician, presided over a decade of meteoric economic growth in the post-Tiananmen era.

2022-11-30.title: A top E.U. official proposes a special court for alleged Russian crimes.
2022-11-30.abstract: President Zelensky, his wife and Ukraine’s prosecutor general all lobbied the international community to support their proposal.

2022-11-30.title: Residents and Police Face Off in China’s Latest Protests
2022-11-30.abstract: Although security forces have reasserted control in many cities, rebellions against Beijing’s strict Covid rules have rocked the southern city of Guangzhou this week.
